summary: Synthetic support interaction with misleading evidence, abandoned paths, conflicting observations, a hypothesis reversal, and a late root-cause reveal.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I need help with something odd. migration completed with no fatal errors.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I need to follow the standard checklist before we discuss escalation. When did this first start?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That points me toward partial data import, but I want to keep this tentative. What changed right before the issue started?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: The confusing part is this: An early symptom seems to support partial data import, but later evidence weakens it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Let's test partial data import first. If that fits, we should see the same behavior on a known-good example.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: We checked one example and it seemed to fit, but then another example did not. migration completed with no fatal errors, but owners cannot edit migrated records.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That weakens partial data import. I am going to abandon that path for now and check workspace storage limit.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: workspace storage limit happened to us once before, so that sounds plausible, but the symptoms are not exactly the same.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Agreed. workspace storage limit is possible, but the evidence is mixed. I need a comparison between a working and blocked case.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: One working case has the same visible setup. Also, a custom legacy owner role existed.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That reverses the direction. The visible setup may not be the control point. I want to inspect the shared configuration boundary.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I just got a note from someone else: migration logs show fallback role mapping. customer has an onboarding review with executives tomorrow.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That late detail finally makes the actual path coherent. The likely root cause is: Legacy project owners were mapped to viewers during import.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: So the earlier checks were not wasted, but they were pointing at the wrong control point?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Correct. The earlier paths were plausible but abandoned after conflicting evidence. Final outcome: agent finds custom legacy role mapping error and schedules corrected reimport
